description: P1 Production Hardening - Pagination, indexes, Docker optimization, SSE keepalive, and cleanup
steps: 1. Add pagination to GET /projects in artifacts/api-server/src/routes/projects.ts: accept ?page=1&limit=20 query params (default page=1, limit=20, max limit=100). Return { data: [...], pagination: { page, limit, total, totalPages } }. Update GET /projects/recent to use limit param.

2. Create a new migration file lib/db/migrations/0007_add_indexes.sql: CREATE INDEX idx_projects_user_id ON projects(user_id); CREATE INDEX idx_projects_status ON projects(status); CREATE INDEX idx_projects_updated_at ON projects(updated_at DESC); -- sessions.expires_at already has index from migration 0005

3. Add SSE heartbeat: in artifacts/api-server/src/routes/projects.ts and web-generate.ts, after flushing headers for SSE endpoints, set up a setInterval that writes `: heartbeat\n\n` every 15 seconds. Clear the interval when the response ends. This keeps proxy connections alive during long AI calls.
4. Create a .dockerignore file at the project root with: node_modules, .git, .env, *.md, .github, attached_assets, .agents, dist, coverage

5. Fix Dockerfile layer caching: copy only pnpm-workspace.yaml, pnpm-lock.yaml, .npmrc, package.json, tsconfig files, and all package.json files from sub-packages FIRST, run pnpm install, THEN copy source code. This ensures code changes don't bust the dependency install cache.
7. Make AI model names configurable via env vars in artifacts/api-server/src/lib/ai-client.ts: read OPENAI_MODEL, GEMINI_MODEL, ANTHROPIC_MODEL from env with current values as defaults.
8. Add database connection pool configuration in lib/db/src/index.ts: set max connections (10), idle timeout (30s), connection timeout (5s).
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/projects returns paginated response with pagination metadata
SSE endpoints emit heartbeat comments during long waits
Dockerfile builds successfully with optimized layer caching
AI models can be overridden via environment variables
All existing tests pass (update any tests affected by pagination response shape)
